From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Malayalam newspaper in India

Deepika

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
|  | |
| Type | Daily Newspaper |
| Format | [Broadsheet](/wiki/Broadsheet "Broadsheet") |
| Owner | Rashtra Deepika Ltd. |
| Founder | [Nidhiry Mani Kathanar](/wiki/Nidhiry_Mani_Kathanar "Nidhiry Mani Kathanar") |
| Editor | George Kudilil |
| Managing editor | Michael Vettickat |
| Founded | 15 April 1887 |
| Language | Malayalam, English |
| Headquarters | [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam") |
| [Circulation](/wiki/Newspaper_circulation "Newspaper circulation") | 5,00,000 daily |
| Readership | 3.0 Million |
| Sister newspapers | Rashtradeepika |
| Website | [www.deepika.com](http://www.deepika.com) |
| Free online archives | [epaper.deepika.com](http://epaper.deepika.com/) |

***Deepika*** (transl. Lantern; known as *Nasrani Deepika* till 1939) is an Indian [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam")-language newspaper, which is one of the oldest [newspapers published in India](/wiki/List_of_newspapers_in_India "List of newspapers in India").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It is a mouthpiece of the [Syro-Malabar Catholic](/wiki/Syro-Malabar_Church "Syro-Malabar Church") Church in the region. Started in 1887, it is the oldest Malayalam newspaper now in circulation.[[2]](#cite_note-2) *Deepika* publishes editions from [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam"), [Kochi](/wiki/Kochi,_India "Kochi, India"), [Kannur](/wiki/Kannur "Kannur"), [Thrissur](/wiki/Thrissur "Thrissur"), [Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram "Thiruvananthapuram") and [Kozhikode](/wiki/Kozhikode "Kozhikode").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deepika_(newspaper)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

*Deepika* newspaper was started in 1887 as *Nasrani Deepika* by a Syrian Catholic priest, [Nidhiry Mani Kathanar](/wiki/Nidhiry_Mani_Kathanar "Nidhiry Mani Kathanar"). Jatiaikya Sangham, an organisation formed with the objective of uniting the Pazhayakoor and Puthenkoor communities among the Christians of Kerala, came up with the idea of a newspaper for all the various Christian communities in Kerala.

Although this project did not work out, the Catholics in this group sought the permission of Mar Marcelinos, the bishop of [Veropoly](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Verapoly "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Verapoly") and started a newspaper. The first issue of the newspaper was published on 15 April 1887. Initially, the newspaper was printed on a wooden printing press at St. Joseph's Printing Press, Mannanam, near [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam"). The first editor-in-chief was [Nidhiry Mani Kathanar](/wiki/Nidhiry_Mani_Kathanar "Nidhiry Mani Kathanar").

Shortly afterwards, the *Nasrani Deepika* newspaper split from the Jatiaikya Sangham and was taken over by the Mannanam Ashram, the religious house of the congregation started by St. [Kuriakose Elias Chavara](/wiki/Kuriakose_Elias_Chavara "Kuriakose Elias Chavara").

Initially published twice a month, *Nasrani Deepika* became a daily newspaper from January 1927, going through various stages of publication, three times a month and three times a week. In 1939, the headquarters was shifted from Mannanam to Kottayam. At this point, the newspaper removed the name *Nasrani* and became just *Deepika*.

In 1989, the *Deepika* daily was transferred to Rashtradeepika Limited, a public limited company with clergy, faithful, directors and shareholders. Later in 2005, the acquisition of a majority stake in the company by some individuals and the forced retirement of permanent employees, including journalists, led to much controversy.

*Deepika* has been accused of extorting money from the CIA by former chief minister [V. S. Achuthanandan](/wiki/V._S._Achuthanandan "V. S. Achuthanandan"). The newspaper announced that the Chief Minister would be paid Rs 1 crore if the allegations are proved.

The Deepika Friends Club, which includes *Deepika* readers, was launched in 2015. Fr. Benny Mundanattu is the Managing Director and Dr. George Kudilil is the Chief Editor of Deepika Group.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8f/Rashtra_Deepika_Ltd._Kottayam.jpg/250px-Rashtra_Deepika_Ltd._Kottayam.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rashtra_Deepika_Ltd._Kottayam.jpg)

Rashtra Deepika Ltd., Kottayam

*Deepika* was successful in initiating many new changes and innovations in [Malayalam journalism](/wiki/Malayalam_journalism "Malayalam journalism"). *Deepika* was and is the most popular newspaper among the Syrian Catholic community. *Deepika* has taken a strong stand for the farmers and the disadvantaged. *Deepika* is credited with being the first Malayalam newspaper to devote an entire page to sports and the first Malayalam daily to launch an internet edition. In April 1992 an evening edition, ***Rashtra Deepika***, was added to the newspaper group.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Currently, *Deepika* owns two news portals, www.deepika.com and www.rashtradeepika.com.

*Deepika* completed 125 years of service in 2012.

| Karshakan | Monthly | [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") | Agricultural Magazine |
| Sthreedhanam | Monthly | [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") | Women's Magazine |
| Rashtra Deepika Cinema | Monthly | [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") | Film Magazine |
| Kuttikalude Deepika | Weekly | [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") | Children's fortnightly |
| Children's Digest | Monthly | [English](/wiki/English_language "English language") | Children's Magazine |

## Publishing Centres

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deepika_(newspaper)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Publishing Centres")]

Deepika publishes editions from :

* Kottayam
* Kochi
* Kannur
* Thrissur
* Thiruvananthapuram
* Kozhikode

• deepikaglobal.com (Online English Newspaper)
